issue_title: Converting Between Scientific Unit Systems w/ Different Dimensions
issue_body:
I'm a physicist who would like to use the nifty package in my simulation codes. However I have an important question.

Often, I have to convert between two unit systems that have fundamentally different notions of what the dimensions of a given quantity are. 

For example, in a project I am currently working on I have to move back and forth between [SI](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SI_base_unit)
and [Lorentz-Heaviside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lorentz%E2%80%93Heaviside_units#Replacing_CGS_with_natural_units) unit systems.
In Lorentz-Heaviside units (because the speed of light c = 1 and is dimensionless), one can consider time to be measured in units of length and frequency to be measured in units of inverse length. 

Is there a way to set up these two unit systems, and use them _simultaneously_? The docs seem to indicate I can define my own unit systems in a text file, but creating two `UnitRegistry` objects and converting `Quantity` objects between the two in the same running application is not allowed. 

This is important for me, because my simulation codes compute things in Lorentz-Heaviside units for proper numerical conditioning, but I analyze and store things in SI units for easy interpretation and reporting. 

Thanks!

issue_title: Modify both span and category of annotation -> only category is changed
issue_body:
I m currently correcting some of my annotations for the PARSEME shared task on FLAT and encountered this problem, which I think is a bug. It is quite straightforward to reproduce:
1. Open a previously annotated file
2. Click on an annotation to open the dialogue, this by default opens direct edition mode
3. Change the category, say from LVC -> ID
4. Exclude a word from the span of annotation by clicking on it
5. Press OK
I expected that the new span of words would get the new category (ID) but only the category is changed. The span remains the same. I need to make a second edition, not touching the category, only including/excluding words, and then it does it. Both types of modifications simultaneously doesn't seem to work.

issue_title: Add environ arg to _locToFilePath for per-user chrooting?
issue_body:
Hi Martin

We run a setup with a custom FilesystemProvider where we want to limit users to their individual home sub-directory inside rootFolderPath. I thought we'd already managed that with the existing wsgidav code, but it turns out we ran into a nasty race when trying to override the lookup function dynamically.

Would it be acceptable for you to change the _locToFilePath method to take not only the path but additionally an environ argument (in symmetry with getResourceInst)?
In that way we only need to override that method once and for all to use the username auth header for the chroot-mapping.

It requires a few call changes throughout fs_dav_provider.py but I can provide a pull request if that's any help?

Cheers, Jonas

    AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'fill'
```

Not sure what causes it. Maybe some sort of race condition? This happens in a background job system which does use threads, so maybe there is a race condition in the library where it removes the self._sock objects from the connection in one thread and tries to use it in another? I'm not totally convinced that's the problem though because it happens in short bursts across multiple webservers. Maybe some sort of network disruption event causes it?

issue_title: Reader(conn, objectDef, base).search() does not (easily) surface SvcErr to caller
issue_body:
I had an issue today where I was reading Users from an ActiveDirectory server. My `reader.search()` was returning an empty list, which didn't make much sense since I knew the query and base work for `connection.search()`. The problem, it turned out, was that the ObjectDef for 'User' included the `msds-memberTransitive` and `msds-memberOfTransitive` attributes which, according to [this stack overflow question](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27315132/error-showing-values-of-fields-msds-memberoftransitive-and-msds-membertransitive) will not be returned for search operations scoped to a single object. That's fine, I suppose, since that's Microsoft's problem.

My issue was that I was completely unaware of this until I turned on `set_library_log_detail_level(EXTENDED)`, which gave me the following
```
<<{'controls': None,
<< 'messageID': 7,
<< 'payload': [(0, False, 10, 1),
<<             (0, False, 4, ''),
<<             (0,
<<              False,
<<              4,
<<              '00002120: SvcErr: DSID-03120435, problem 5012 (DIR_ERROR), data 592060\n\x00')],
<< 'protocolOp': 5}
DEBUG:ldap3:BASIC:done SEARCH operation, result <False>
```

Since `reader.search()` returns the resulting entries (in this case empty list) instead of True/False like `connection.search()` I was unable to detect the issue without visual inspection of the log.

Is there a way this could somehow be made detectable (like raising an exception or something)?

- CCBlock
- CABlock
- ATBlock

I didn't file a pull request for this issue, as I don't know if this has negative effects in some cases.

issue_title: Compiling Detailed Play by Play Data
issue_body:
Hey guys,

So I've tried to navigate the raw JSON data but I had a load of trouble with formatting with what I was using.

For me the easiest way of navigating the data is if it's in CSV format. So I want to get each play in a game as well as detailed stats at the time of the play like score/players/yards on the play/ect.

Is there an easier way of doing this apart from going through the directories to find a specific field?

Thanks in advance!

* Nipyapi version: 0.9.1
* NiFi version: 1.6.0/1.7.0
* NiFi-Registry version: N/A
* Python version: 3.6.3
* Operating System: CentOS 7.4.1708

### Description

In our current docker build/setup we are experiencing some issues with proper policies rights being set. We sought to use nipyapi to correct that. While learning/understanding how to create/modify/delete policy information in NiFi we attempted to use the nipyapi function "nipyapi.security.get_access_policy_for_resource" to view existing information. However using the call, regardless of the variations in options, fails to complete, errors on "TypeError: Got an unexpected keyword argument 'id' to method get_access_policy_for_resource".


### What I Did

The test script:
```
nipyapi.nifi.configuration.verify_ssl = False
nipyapi.config.nifi_config.host = 'https://nifi:9443/nifi-api'
nipyapi.security.set_service_ssl_context(
    service='nifi',
    ca_file='nifi-cert.pem',
    client_cert_file='client-cert.pem',
    client_key_file='client-key.key',
    client_key_password='supersecretpassword'
)
canvas_root_policy = nipyapi.security.get_access_policy_for_resource('flow', 'read')
print(canvas_root_policy)
```

Fails with the following output:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./test.py", line 37, in <module>
    canvas_root_policy = nipyapi.security.get_access_policy_for_resource('flow', 'read')
  File "/opt/cgimss-utils/lib/python3.6/site-packages/nipyapi/security.py", line 367, in get_access_policy_for_resource
    id=r_id
  File "/opt/cgimss-utils/lib/python3.6/site-packages/nipyapi/nifi/apis/policies_api.py", line 279, in get_access_policy_for_resource
    (data) = self.get_access_policy_for_resource_with_http_info(action, resource, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/cgimss-utils/lib/python3.6/site-packages/nipyapi/nifi/apis/policies_api.py", line 314, in get_access_policy_for_resource_with_http_info
    " to method get_access_policy_for_resource" % key
TypeError: Got an unexpected keyword argument 'id' to method get_access_policy_for_resource
```


When lookin into the code a bit we can see the call being made to "nipyapi.nifi.PoliciesApi().get_access_policy_for_resource" contains the keyword "id". Following it down to where the error comes from it appears it is just because that key is not listed in "all_params" on line 303 of "policies_api.py". Adding it correct's the behavior, though may not be the right way to fix the issue. It might also appear in other similar calls?

nipyapi/security.py
```
    360     try:
    361         if service == 'nifi':
    362             log.info("Getting NiFi policy for %s:%s/%s",
    363                      action, resource, r_id)
    364             return nipyapi.nifi.PoliciesApi().get_access_policy_for_resource(
    365                 action=action,
    366                 resource=resource,
    367                 id=r_id
    368             )
```
nipyapi/nifi/apis/policies_api.py
```
    303         all_params = ['action', 'resource']
    304         all_params.append('callback')
    305         all_params.append('_return_http_data_only')
    306         all_params.append('_preload_content')
    307         all_params.append('_request_timeout')
    308
    309         params = locals()
    310         for key, val in iteritems(params['kwargs']):
    311             if key not in all_params:
    312                 raise TypeError(
    313                     "Got an unexpected keyword argument '%s'"
    314                     " to method get_access_policy_for_resource" % key
    315                 )
    316             params[key] = val
    317         del params['kwargs']
```

### Urgency

Currently in development, nothing is in production today. Started to use nipyapi to automate some initial deployment tasks. We encountering issues with Docker NiFi and policies, hoping to fix them with nipyapi. There are manual work arounds for the time being.

issue_title: Use new features from latest Cython
issue_body:
In Cython 0.20 added support for calling C++ template functions. We already use some C++ template functions, but now it can be done in a more convenient way, the same way importing of C++ template classes work.

In Cython 0.21 enums can now be declared as cpdef to export their values to the module's Python namespace. Cpdef enums in pxd files export their values to their own module, if it exists. Reference: https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/c67e895414aac90dfe9f789530143cff5b2ec7ad/CHANGES.rst#021-2014-09-10

I just noticed a small problem with overlapping trades. Specifically, if trade 0 currently has an open long position with size 1, and then trade 1 started a sell order with size 1 and the order got executed. A while later, the trade 1 sent out another buy order to close the short position and the order got executed. Note that the trade 0 still remains open with long position. Internally this buy order was labeled as "opened". This is because positions from all three orders were aggregated together, and the net position is long with size 1. But then in strategy.py, from line 382 to line 390, even though the trade 1 is actually closed, the current code failed to add it to the pending trade list, so the notify_trade() function will not be called. It seems the PnL calculation doesn't get affected by this small problem. I tried to add the following two lines of code at line 389 right after the trade.update() call:
                if trade.isclosed:
                    self._tradespending.append(trade)

It seems to fix it. I just wonder whether you see any potential problems with this change.

Hi, I think the usage of this library could be simpler:

```python
import json262 as json

#... old code comes here (unchanged) :-)
```

I don't know it is difficult to implement it this way.

Or do reasons exist why this is not a good idea?

I have been trying to debug some issues with Home Assistant and I traced it back down to the is_active_input parameter in the settings. I am not entirely sure what this value is supposed to represent, but I am assuming that it is intended to report if the Chromecast is on the TV's selected input.

For me, it appears that the value reported from the Chromecast is changing at random for systems that I believe to support CEC. Sometimes it works, other times it doesn't. For systems that I know do not support CEC, it appears to not be provided.

This value eventually propagates down to the is_idle property. That value is then used inside of Home Assistant for the Cast's status. When this value is not properly functioning, and an unsupported application (Netflix) is playing, HA will report Off instead of Unknown.

I don't know what anyone else's experience is with this parameter, but, assuming I have the proper understanding of its purpose, it seems buggy for me. I would contend that maybe this parameter should not be considered when calculating is_idle since maybe this parameter should only answer the question of is the Chromecast itself idle. I'm not sure it would be best to have this parameter indicate the status of other devices it is connected to.

Then again, I may not be correctly interpreting the purpose of this value.

您好！用了comiccrawler抓了那麼多圖，飆了那麼多車。十分感謝作者編寫的軟體帶來的便利！故有幾個問題，或者說是提議請指教。

1.有關於文件命名！有時候在同一頁網頁抓圖時，up主有時會把兩個相同文件名的圖片放在同一頁，例如001.png，002.png【黑】，003.png，002.png【白】，004.png，005.png......但其中命名為002的兩個文件並不是同一個文件，由於在同一目錄下，軟體抓圖下來時，會用後面的img_002.png【白】覆蓋掉前面已經抓下的img_002.png【黑】。請問能不能實現在同一目錄下，當抓到同一文件名但卻是不同文件的時候，在後抓的圖命名上加上副標題，即002(1).png。這個類似PC同一目錄下，重複文件就在原文件名後添加副標題的重命名。請問可不可以實現？

2.有關於圖源的身份驗證。有些圖源網站是有地區IP限制的，紳士往往需要掛代理才能抓圖。由於網絡環境較差[代理有時當機重啟]，以及原圖源數量過於多，單文件過大[高清大圖通常上數MB]，會造成抓圖不成功。例如抓個4MB的原圖，突然網絡延遲或斷聯了後重聯，軟體就只抓2.33MB的原圖就跳過抓下一個圖了，造成原本4MB的原圖只有2.33MB的壞檔。2.33MB圖片雖然可以打開，但只顯示一半，下半部是灰的一片。我覺得可以參考hash驗證這種方法，例如從網盤下載某分享文件，up主貼出hash值，在紳士下載完這個文件後，校驗hash值，對比up主貼出的hash，確保文件完整性。類比comiccrawler，可以加入這個機制，抓完一張圖，本地與網源同時對比hash，確保圖源完整性，是就跳到下一張圖，否就自動刪除壞檔重新下載。這樣不用在抓完一堆CG後，還要一張張視察。作者您覺得如何？

3.翻看了G託管的許多討論，其中有說到是否需要在軟體中添加代理，個人覺得沒有必要。在使用代理軟體時，設置全局代理，再啟用comiccrawler時就可以規避IP地區限制了，前提是代理的IP在允許的範圍內。推薦影梭，但需要VPS，具體使用及說明就不多說了，作者可以多了解。

說了那麼多廢話，不好意思呢！謝謝閱讀！望comiccralwer功能更強大，使用更方便！

另，不知為何博客無法留言，只能發到這。謝謝！

issue_body:
This is proposal, not a bug.

Currently, the only way to run the test I am aware of is by `$ python setup.py test`.

I do not know, if one can run the test selecting only specific test (what would speed up development of particular features).

Rewrite to `pytest` would result in:
- moving all existing tests into `tests` folder (preserve all existing test cases)
- more modular test suite code
- nicer problem report in case of failure (nicely coloured, more expressive)
- easy option to discover existing tests without running them
- easy option to run just particular test
- allow coverage report

I am asking for an opinion before I dive into it as it takes few hours of work.

According to [the docs](http://api.mongodb.com/python/current/api/gridfs/#gridfs.GridFS), with PyMongo 3.1, GridFS will only create the indexes on a first write. This behavior is [specified by design](https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/gridfs/gridfs-spec.rst#indexes). Therefore, routines like `move-gridfs` won't work on empty destinations. The routine should account for this and ensure the default indexes are created.

`type` as an `array` or a `dict`, sort of like how `secondary_y` works:

df = pd.DataFrame({

MySQL's string comparison is case-insensitive by default ( https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.5/en/case-sensitivity.html ). None of Django's default lookups allow you to escape this without changing the column to a binary collation ( https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/db/backends/mysql/base.py#L201). I imagine a lookup like `field__case_exact=BLA` would be good to implement and add to CharField, TextField, etc.

issue_title: Captured log is printed twice if there's an error in the test and during teardown
issue_body:
When there's a situation where we have an error in a test *and* during teardown:

```python
import logging
import pytest

@pytest.yield_fixture
def fixt():
    yield
    raise Exception


def test_foo(fixt):
    logging.debug("Hello World")
    raise ValueError
```

Then the log is printed twice:

```
=========================== ERRORS ===========================
_______________ ERROR at teardown of test_foo ________________

    [...]

logtest.py:7: Exception
--------------------- Captured log call ----------------------
logtest.py                  11 DEBUG    Hello World
========================== FAILURES ==========================
__________________________ test_foo __________________________

    [...]

logtest.py:12: ValueError
--------------------- Captured log call ----------------------
logtest.py                  11 DEBUG    Hello World
```

Some background:

This happens quite often for me, as I start a subprocess in a test and parse its output. When there's an unparsable line, I want to fail the test but continue so I get more output (imagine e.g. a traceback - I don't want to abort the test on the first unparsable line as there's more useful information to come). So what I do is to set a flag, and raise an error during teardown (in a fixture) when there's an unparseable line.

Now if there is something like that, chances are the test itself will also have failed in some way, and I get the whole debug log (often dozens to hundreds of lines) twice.

While trying to filter on a multi-select field in my admin, I noticed that very little results were being returned. The lookup being used is using `__exact` but in my use case, I want it to be `__contains`, as I want to see everyone who answered _at least_ the option I've selected.

My solution to fix that was to add to my codebase the following bit of code:
```python
class MultipleChoicesFieldListFilter(admin.ChoicesFieldListFilter):
    """
    List filter for MultiSelectField, which overrides the lookup to
    use __contains instead of __exact
    """
    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
        super().__init__(field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path)
        self.lookup_kwarg = '%s__contains' % field_path
        self.lookup_val = request.GET.get(self.lookup_kwarg)


# Override existing registration
FieldListFilter.register(
    lambda f: isinstance(f, MultiSelectField),
    MultipleChoicesFieldListFilter,
    take_priority=True,
)
```

Would you be interested in seeing this added to the library? I've seen #49, #54  and #59 which seem related. 

My concern with this implementation is that if an option (e.g. "car") is is a substring of another (e.g. "supercar"), filtering for "car" might filter contains items which have only "supercar" in it.

issue_title: tearDown does not rollback the test data before to migrate back to latest
issue_body:
Let assume we want a migration to rather fail when the data is not correct (Ex: require human intervention to fix the data) and we want to test that.

1. We create test data
2. Call `self.run_migration()`
3. Migration throws exception
4. We catch the exception and validation data rolled-back successfully, test succeeds so far (everything is fine)
5. Then teardown run and exception is thrown. Test fail.
6. Database is now in a bad state

The teardown of `MigrationTest` will run the migration again to restore the previous state of the migration which will fail since the test data will be still there.

The workaround is to do the teardown of the data ourself but it is impractical, I would assume `MigrationTest` would clear the test data before to roll the migrations.

issue_title: Null value for Generic Relation causes Invalid Model Error
issue_body:
I have a number of GenericForeignKey relations on my model and they are all permitted to be nulls.

I find that `determine_deserializer_for_data` throws an error when trying to figure out the class for the specific value when it is null. This can be fixed (hopefully without causing issues elsewhere) as follows by adding a quick `if`:

    def field_to_native(self, obj, field_name):
        """
        Delegates to the `to_native` method of the serializer registered
        under obj.__class__
        """
        value = super(GenericRelatedField, self).field_to_native(
            obj, field_name)
        if value:
            serializer = self.determine_deserializer_for_data(value)
    
            # Necessary because of context, field resolving etc.
            serializer.initialize(self.parent, field_name)
            return serializer.to_native(value)

issue_title: Reads returning old document versions when reusing client
issue_body:
I have a serverless function calling a Cloudant database via the [python-cloudant][1] library, with the connection to the client being made outside the function handler so that it is cached, like so:

    httpAdapter = HTTPAdapter(pool_connections=5, pool_maxsize=100)
    client = Cloudant(cloudantUsername, cloudantPassword, url=cloudantURL, adapter=httpAdapter, connect=True)

    def update(event, context):
        db = client['db_name']
        document_id = event['pathParameters']['id']
        document = db[document_id]
        document['foo'] = 'bar'
        document.save()
        return json.dumps(document)

    def get(event, context):
        db = client['db_name']
        document_id = event['pathParameters']['id']
        document = db[document_id]
        return json.dumps(document)

When I do this, I get a very low latency (<100ms) on repeat executions of any kind (create, update, get), because the "connection" is reused, which is the intention. ("connection" in inverted commas since I know cloudant does not have a concept of keeping an open pipe).

If I call `/update` and increment the document `_rev` to `2-XXXX` then call `/get`, the function returns document with `_rev` `2-XXXX` - expected behaviour. 

If I call `/update` again, `_rev` is correctly incremented to `3-XXXX`, but when I call `/get` again, I get document revision `2-XXXX` (i.e. not the updated version). When this is happening, the update function is executing in ~90ms, while the get is executing in ~10ms (too fast to actually be calling the database).

I can eliminate this behaviour by either moving the setup of the Cloudant client inside the function handler like so:

    httpAdapter = HTTPAdapter(pool_connections=5, pool_maxsize=100)

    def update(event, context):
        client = Cloudant(cloudantUsername, cloudantPassword, url=cloudantURL, adapter=httpAdapter, connect=True)
        db = client['db_name']
        document_id = event['pathParameters']['id']
        document = db[document_id]
        document['foo'] = 'bar'
        document.save()
        return json.dumps(document)

    def get(event, context):
        client = Cloudant(cloudantUsername, cloudantPassword, url=cloudantURL, adapter=httpAdapter, connect=True)
        db = client['db_name']
        document_id = event['pathParameters']['id']
        document = db[document_id]
        return json.dumps(document)

or I can explicitly call `document.fetch()` in the get handler:

    def get(event, context):
        db = client['db_name']
        document_id = event['pathParameters']['id']
        document = db[document_id]
        document.fetch()
        return json.dumps(document)

In either case, `/get` always returns the correct revision of the document.

Getting an old version of the document is unexpected since I believed putting the client setup outside the handler would only cache the client between executions, but calls to the database would still be made each time the handler is called. It looks like they are being made when `/update` is called, presumably since `document.save()` forces it, but not when `/get` is called without a forced call with `.fetch()`.

Is putting the client declaration outside the handler somehow caching the database? If so, is there any way to prevent it from doing so? Are context managers required in this scenario?

  [1]: https://github.com/cloudant/python-cloudant

Simply, gevent does not support py3 at the moment (see gevent/gevent#38, despite the many years effort it is still not here).
Incidentally, pyzmq can't unfortunately claim python 3 support when using greenlets :(. Could you add the exception to the readme?

(btw, why not using eventlet instead of gevent? One can do 'from eventlet.green import zmq', but it is a bit of a shame to have to use a zmq greenlet implementation from another package when pyzmq is already supposed to provide one).

issue_title: Provide way to add links outside of item definitions
issue_body:
Our specific use case:

Want to link SWRQT items (split over multiple files) to ITEST items (all in a single autogenerated file). 

- As the ITEST items are autogenerated, we can't add links in the item definition without adapting the generated file.
- Would like to avoid adding the link in the SWRQT item definition, as these requirements need to be easily reusable for other projects (with a different set of ITEST items)

A proposal would be to add an additional directive, like so:

```
.. item-link::
    :source: SWRQT-flits
    :targets: ITEST-flats
    :type: verified_by
```

This could then be maintained in a different file, easily swappable per project

issue_title: Use a 96-bit nonce for the IV
issue_body:
Currently your code uses a 256-bit random IV (nonce) for AES-GCM. GCM is designed to use a 96-bit nonce and if you give it a longer (or shorter) value it hashes it to obtain a value of the correct length (see Algorithm 4 of [NIST SP.800-38D](http://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/Legacy/SP/nistspecialpublication800-38d.pdf). As you are using random IVs anyway, this doesn't change much from a security perspective, but it is inefficient to use a 256-bit value here when 96-bits is all that is required (after base64-encoding you are adding about an extra 27 bytes to each encrypted value).

Note that the IV for GCM does not need to be random, it just needs to never repeat for the same key (a nonce: see the Deterministic IV Construction in Section 8.2.1 of the linked specification). As you are generating fresh random 256-bit AES keys for each file and processing the whole file at once, I would suggest just keeping a simple counter that you increment for each value that you encrypt in the file: 0 for the first, 1 for the second etc. Encode this as a 96-bit value (e.g. big-endian) and use that as the nonce. That way you do not need to explicitly record the IV at all.

issue_title: Make it easy to aggregate the asset_loss_table by tag
issue_body:
We will need to change the way it is stored. I should become a table of shape (A, E) where A is the number of assets, E the number of events and of dtype `('rlzi', U16), ('loss', (F32, (self.L * self.I,))`. Then it will be easy to aggregate the losses by using the taskmask.
Notice that this approach will not work for UCERF, since storing the full asset loss table is not an option there. Still, we could aggregate by tag there and in general we could only store a table of shape (T, E) where T is the number of tags.

Currently, even if the user has selected a specific disaggregation strategy, the engine performs the disaggregation for all strategies and consumes a lot of memory. We should improve on that. Typical strategies are `mag_dist` and `mag_dist_eps`, perhaps some further optimizations can be done for those cases.

issue_title: create_instances() doesn't clean up after itself if interrupted
issue_body:
Say [`create_instances()`](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.ServiceResource.create_instances) is in the middle of its work and the user interrupts it with a `KeyboardInterrupt`. What's the recommended way to ensure that no orphaned instances are left behind?

From what I am seeing, it looks like there's no way to programmatically capture the instances that got created (if any) before `create_instances()` was interrupted. Is that correct? In other words, your program exits and you have no way of automatically cleaning up any resources that may have been left behind.

I tried to do a parallel scan spawning multiple workers using the multiprocessing lib.

However, I found out that one of the workers is always that is scanning the whole table.

Executing returns the following:
```
> work(worker, (), pool_size=4)
Total for seg 2: 1313
Total for seg 3: 1329
Total for seg 1: 1344
Total for seg 0: 5239
Final total count: 9225
```

I ran this for different tables and its always the same issue, worker 0 scans the whole table, producing duplicated items in the final result collection. Also, I tried to use `map_asych` instead but had the same issue.

Is this an internal boto3 issue? Or is there anything wrong with the `read_table` function?

I'm running:
- boto3 v1.4.7
- python: 3.6.3


Thanks in advance
---
The used code:
```python
import boto3
import multiprocessing as mp

def read_table(table_name, pe, seg=None, total_seg=None, limit=None):
    dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb')
    scan_args = {
        'TableName': table_name,
        'ProjectionExpression': pe
    }
    if seg and total_seg:
        scan_args['TotalSegments'] = total_seg
        scan_args['Segment'] = seg
    if limit:
        scan_args['Limit'] = limit

    items = []
    total_scanned_count = 0
    while True:
        response = dynamodb.scan(**scan_args)
        items += response['Items']
        total_scanned_count += response['ScannedCount']
        if limit:
            scan_args['Limit'] = limit - total_scanned_count
        
        if 'LastEvaluatedKey' not in response or limit == total_scanned_count:
            break

        scan_args['ExclusiveStartKey'] = response['LastEvaluatedKey']
                   
    print("Total for seg {}: {}".format(seg, len(items)))

    return items


def worker(seg=None, total_seg=None):
    print("Running on segment {}".format(seg))
    items = read_table('table-name', 'id', seg=seg, total_seg=total_seg)
    
    return items


def work(worker, args=(), pool_size=mp.cpu_count()):
    pool = mp.Pool(processes=pool_size)

    result = []
    for i in range(pool_size):
        callback = lambda x: result.append(x)
        error_callback = lambda x: print(x)
        kwds = {'seg': i, 'total_seg': pool_size}
        pool.apply_async(worker, args=args, kwds=kwds, callback=callback,
                         error_callback=error_callback)

    pool.close()
    pool.join()

    return result
```

issue_title: pipenv 11.10.1 fails when there are 3 or more [[source]] entries in a Pipfile
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues (both open and closed!).

Describe the issue briefly here.
pipenv 11.10.1 fails when the Pipfile contains 3 or more source entries.

issue_title: 'pipenv install' fails with 'ssh: Could not resolve hostname my.domain.com:gitlab_username Name or service not known'
issue_body:
##### Issue description

'pipenv install' fails since version 2018.6.25 if the Pipfile contains a package reference to a Gitlab repository, like e.g.: `my_package = {git = "git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git", ref = 
"master"}`

Here, my.domain.com is a replacement for the properly resolving domain.

_Downgrading to 2018.5.18 resolves the issue._


##### Expected result

It should install 'my_package'.

##### Actual result

```
Obtaining my_package from git+ssh://git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git@856f8ff9146dc7fbfc1ae3595203a9f3af8cf023#egg=my_package
  Cloning ssh://git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git (to revision 856f8ff9146dc7fbfc1ae3595203a9f3af8cf023) to /home/me/.local/share/virtualenvs/my_project-WRKZN-2y/src/my_package
ssh: Could not resolve hostname my.domain.com:gitlab_username: Name or service not known
fatal: Could not read from remote repository.

Please make sure you have the correct access rights
and the repository exists.

Command "git clone -q ssh://git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git /home/me/.local/share/virtualenvs/my_project-WRKZN-2y/src/my_package" failed with error code 128 in None
```

pipenv seems to append ':gitlab_username' to the domain name and as a result fails to resolve the hostname.


##### Steps to replicate

Replace git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git correspondingly and run `pipenv install` 

```toml
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[requires]
python_version = "2.7"

[dev-packages]

[packages]
my_package = {ref = "v1.0.0", git = "git@my.domain.com:gitlab_username/my_package.git", editable = true}
```

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

<details><summary>$ pipenv --support</summary>

Pipenv version: `'2018.7.1'`

Pipenv location: `'/home/me/.local/share/virtualenvs/my_project-WRKZN-2y/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pipenv'`

Python location: `'/home/me/.local/share/virtualenvs/my_project-WRKZN-2y/bin/python2.7'`

Other Python installations in `PATH`:

  - `2.7`: `/home/me/.local/share/virtualenvs/my_project-WRKZN-2y/bin/python2.7`

```

</details>

For one of the projects I work on (Astropy) we have been making use of code that can be simplified to:

```python
import pkg_resources
from setuptools import Distribution


class CustomDistribution(Distribution):
    def get_option_dict(self, command_name):
        opts = Distribution.get_option_dict(self, command_name)
        if command_name == 'easy_install':
            opts['find_links'] = ('setup script', ['astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz'])
            opts['allow_hosts'] = ('setup script', '')
        return opts


CustomDistribution(attrs={'setup_requires': ['astropy-helpers']})

print(pkg_resources.working_set.by_key.get('astropy-helpers'))
```

This requires the following tar file to run: [astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/46/49/4882dc7b3fc1c47d481db229777a82e74751e6a1f0a4af90d9df5285bb21/astropy-helpers-2.0.3.tar.gz)

Before https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150, this would cause astropy-helpers to be installed to the ``.eggs`` directory using the local tar file, but since the changes in https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150 this no longer works (in the above example, the last line used to return a distribution object, but astropy-helpers is now no longer in the ``working_set`` so this returns ``None``)

Is this an unintended consequence of https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/1150, or should we update our code above for it to continue working?

cc @benoit-pierre @jaraco

I'm a Buildroot user and after bumping setuptools to 36.0.1 we now experience issues with Python 2.7.x and packages that need a CFFI backend. You can find more details on the [CFFI mailing list](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/python-cffi/ttSTbCpnDEo/7dkPw8fOAQAJ).

Buildroot (BR) has so called host-python, that is compiled for the host BR is running on and target-python, that is part of the target root file system. During target package setup we augment PYTHONPATH with target/usr/lib/python2.7/sysconfigdata and target/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages, so that setup.py could have access to other target python packages.

In the case of CFFI it is important, that _cffi_backend will be imported in the host-variant otherwise we get:

ImportError: /home/peko/autobuild/instance-2/output/target/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/_cffi_backend.so: wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32

In setuptools version 18.7.1 put cffi-1.10.0-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg before our target/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages, so that host-variant could be imported, but setuptools 36.0.1 makes sys.path ordering differently and cffi-1.10.0-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg lands at the end of the list.  The ordering can be taken from the above mentioned discussion on the CFFI mailing list.

How one can enforce another sys.path order?

issue_title: Use last object if NextMarker is not set and IsTruncated is true
issue_body: NextMarker may not be populated, use the last object seen if this is the case.

Current implementation of language detection works in a way *"When we ask for next language, that means that previous language did not work on the given date and should be dropped"* We need to change this behavior to only modify working set of languages if at least one language was applicable. If possible, we should also find a way to make language detection more clear for the reader.
This need to be done without significant increase of code complexity.
Current test case is:
```ipython
In [1]: from dateparser import DateDataParser
In [2]: parser = DateDataParser()
In [3]: parser.get_date_data(u'01-01-15 06:47 AM')
Out[3]: {'date_obj': datetime.datetime(2015, 1, 1, 6, 47), 'period': 'day'}
In [4]: parser.get_date_data(u'foo')
Out[4]: {'date_obj': None, 'period': 'day'}
In [5]: parser.get_date_data(u'01-01-15 06:47 AM')
Out[5]: {'date_obj': None, 'period': 'day'}
```

issue_title: Configurable log file name and format
issue_body: Currently the scrapy log file generated by scrapyd is named as [job's id](https://github.com/scrapy/scrapyd/blob/master/scrapyd/environ.py#L63) and format is hardcoded as [`.log`](https://github.com/scrapy/scrapyd/blob/master/scrapyd/environ.py#L35), similar case also happens on items file. It would be better if we could customize those file names and formats, like, if we wish to include spider's name or timestamp in a log name and compress it into `zip` format to save space.

KeyError: 'fallback' at parler/cache.py in _get_cached_values, line 126
Apparently, if we have something like `{'code': 'de', 'fallbacks': ['en', 'fr', 'it']},..` `_get_cached_values()` fails.

This is in fact part of the problem for #129. When I have Some model which is related to Translation and not to Master then this error occurs.

    AttributeError: 'ManyToOneRel' object has no attribute 'get_attname'

Making it possible to access relations to translation instead of master is hard, but for now not preventing creation of objects with other fields is a start.

I'm preparing a pull request for this.

Giving the whole logging thing a bit more thought here's what I think would be ideal:

* no print sys.stderr calls, everything should use logging
* calls to `track()`, `identify()`, etc… should check if the library is configured right away and log to `ERROR` if it isn't however this shouldn't cause the call to return. It should still queue the data and behave as it normally would.
* failed preconditions should use python's `assert` for internal checks/safeguards and raise `ValueError` if something is called improperly by the calling developer (with a missing `event` string for example). This gives the developer the chance to catch `ValueError`s and not have to catch `Exception` which is too general.
* All calls to `track`, `identify`, etc... should cause an `on_success` or `on_failure` callback, not matter what. Ideally the call will be queued just like normal and then the sync thread would decide if it is properly configured and can send to the server or not. This allows a developer to check their code and print/debug even if they don't have a segment.io key.
* In general, reduce the chance that calls to identify/track will raise exceptions on the request handling thread.

The main issue is that not all developers on a team should be sending data to segment.io but they should still be a) testing as much of the identify/track/queue/sync pathway as possible and b) have the option to receive the callbacks `on_failure` and `on_success` so they can see and test what *would* have been sent.

I just want to get these ideas down here for discussion.

Since I cannot provide code directly (as I'm too familiar with the other Healpix implementations, which are under GPL), I thought it might still be useful to point out a few places where astropy-healpix is unnecessarily slow or inaccurate and give hints to improve this. If it's OK with you I'll collect a few of them here.

- determining whether an integer `n` is a power of 2:
  This is best tested by the expression
  `(n>0) && ((n&(n-1))==0)`
  You don't get much faster than this, and since this test is carried out on entry to most functions, it could be a big improvement.

- computing the angle between two 3-vectors `a` and `b`:
  I strongly recommend to use
  `atan2 (length(crossprod(a,b)), dotprod(a,b))`
  This is very accurate for all angles (no loss of precision for angles near 0 and pi), doesn't require normalization and only needs one trigonometric function evaluation.

- converting a 3-vector  `a` to co-latitude and longitude:
  ```
  theta = atan2(sqrt(a.x*a.x+a.y*a.y),a.z);
  phi = atan2 (a.y,a.x);
  ```
- generally: avoid `asin` and `acos` in favor of `atan2` whenever possible.

- `nested_to_xy()` and `xy_to_nested()`
  instead of performing the conversions bit by bit, using lookup tables helps performance immensely, even if they only have 256 entries.
  Modern CPUs even have dedicated machine instructions which perform the whole bit-twiddling step in a few cycles. Healpix C++ will use them when available.

I'll add more points when I come across any!

In the process of moving Invenio documentation from wiki and webdoc to
reST and readthedocs, we should streamline the documentation structure
to better tailor it for various audiences.  Let's take search as an
example: (1) a user may come to docs to learn about advanced search
syntax specifics, (2) an administrator can come to learn about how to
configure default sorting options or facets for various collections;
(3) a developer can come to learn more about XML API internals.

There are basically two different takes on this: (a) we can publish
separate guides for users, admins, developers, and various tutorials;
(b) we can keep one single guide, but structure it better.  Here is
one possibility of such structuring:

```
 1. Introduction

   1.1. What is Invenio?
   1.2. Features
   1.3. Demo
   1.4. Community
   1.5. Release numbering

 2. User Guide

   2.1. Common concepts

   2.4. Submit guide
   2.5. Tagging guide

 3. Installation Guide

   3.1. Quick Install

     3.3.1. Debian

     3.3.3. MacOSX

     3.4.1. First steps

   4.1. Creating site-specific repository

   4.3. Customising back-end code

 5. Configuration Guide

   5.1. Module overview
   5.2. Configure search

   5.4. Configure foo
   5.4. Configure bar

 6. API Guide

   6.1. Record API

   6.4. Tagging API

 7. Development Guide

   7.1. Development practices
     7.1.1. Ten Rules of Contributing

     7.1.3. Coding standards

issue_body:
When building Invenio 3 demo site [using docker](http://invenio.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/installation-quick.html#using-docker), the system does not pass the JSON Schema validation step:

```
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jsonschema/validators.py", line 122, in validate
    for error in self.iter_errors(*args, **kwargs):
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jsonschema/validators.py", line 98, in iter_errors
    for error in errors:
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jsonschema/_validators.py", line 199, in ref
    scope, resolved = validator.resolver.resolve(ref)
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jsonschema/validators.py", line 336, in resolve
    return url, self._remote_cache(url)
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/functools32/functools32.py", line 400, in wrapper
    result = user_function(*args, **kwds)
  File "/home/invenio/.virtualenvs/invenio3/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jsonschema/validators.py", line 346, in resolve_from_url
    raise RefResolutionError(exc)
jsonschema.exceptions.RefResolutionError: HTTPConnectionPool(host='127.0.0.1', port=80): Max retries exceeded with url: /schema/marc21/bibliographic/bd-v1.0.0.json (Caused by NewConnectionError('<requests.packages.urllib3.connection.HTTPConnection object at 0x7fbd19946dd0>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 111] Connection refused',))
```

The web container does not have access to the nginx container.

Ideally, the schemas can be checked locally, not needing the HTTP access. This is connected to `JSONSCHEMAS_HOST` settings that use 127.0.0.1 when running via docker:

```console
$ git grep SCHEMA scripts/
scripts/create-instance.sh:JSONSCHEMAS_CONF=`cat <<EOF
scripts/create-instance.sh:JSONSCHEMAS_ENDPOINT='/schema'
scripts/create-instance.sh:JSONSCHEMAS_HOST='http://${INVENIO_WEB_HOST}'
scripts/create-instance.sh:echo "${JSONSCHEMAS_CONF}" >> ../../var/${INVENIO_WEB_INSTANCE}-instance/${INVENIO_WEB_INSTANCE}.cfg
scripts/populate-instance.sh:SCHEMA="http://${INVENIO_WEB_HOST}/schema/marc21/bibliographic/bd-v1.0.0.json"
scripts/populate-instance.sh:demouuids=$(dojson -i $demomarc21pathname -l marcxml do marc21 schema ${SCHEMA} | \
```

(IIRC I tried to use `localhost` in the past but it wasn't recognised either.)

One option would be to try to supply real IP address, but the best would be to simply recognise addresses such as `0.0.0.0`, `127.0.0.1`, `localhost` as being local, and do the schema validation in Python, not having to access the schemas over the web at all.

P.S. Centralising the ticket here, but we can solve it in the appropriate repository, depending on the chosen method.

issue_title: des MEDS writing row,col convention mismatch
issue_body:
The convention in MEDS is that row,col corresponds to x,y in the image in the following way
```
row -> y
col -> x
```
At these lines they are swapped

https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/master/galsim/des/des_meds.py#L258

Those should read
```python
            dudrow[i] = obj.wcs[i].dudy
            dudcol[i] = obj.wcs[i].dudx
            dvdrow[i] = obj.wcs[i].dvdy
            dvdcol[i] = obj.wcs[i].dvdx
            row0[i]   = obj.wcs[i].origin.y
            col0[i]   = obj.wcs[i].origin.x
```

issue_title: Use fast exp, fast inverse sqrt, etc. when possible
issue_body:
@esheldon and I have been discussing some ways that the k-space rendering in GalSim could be sped up.  One of the ideas is to use faster exp and invsqrt functions when possible.  We have parameters xvalue_accuracy and kvalue_accuracy that tell how accurate we need to be.  I suspect in many cases, we can use quick and dirty versions of various functions that are faster than the native functions.  

e.g. the famous [Quake inverse sqrt](https://www.beyond3d.com/content/articles/8/) (although the [SSE inverse sqrt is probably faster nowadays](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/15175654/sqrt-vs-rsqrt-vs-sse-mm-rsqrt-ps-benchmark)).

Another thought I had was possibly using SSE commands in the fillKValue functions.  Some of the profile types may be amenable to this.  Particularly Exponential if we're able to use rsqrtps.

Anyway, this issue is basically to profile and optimize the drawK operations for Exponential, Spergel, and maybe a few other classes.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13110258/8421449/bba6cb8a-1e9c-11e5-90d8-b889ca431595.png)

TL;DR 
Xcode and iOS require the font's full name to match font-family names.

I spent a couple hours this morning trying to get Font Awesome 5 Pro Light & Light working in Cordova for iOS and ran into an issue where the full name didn't match the css font-family: preventing it from being used in xcode.

The CSS name in `5.0.0-rc2` is `Font Awesome 5 Light` vs the font's full name `Font Awesome 5 Pro Light` - missing 'Pro'. This prevents Xcode and iOS from correctly connecting the two and results in a Error `An error occurred trying to load the resource.`.

import logging

from cbapi.response import *

<generator object netconns at 0x107bfda40>

issue_title: Py-ART ideas from the 2015 AMS radar conference
issue_body:
This is a meta-issue for keeping track of ideas that were discussed with Py-ART users and supporters at the 2015 AMS radar conference.  Expect this top level comment to be edited to reflect changes as development progressed.  Some of these issue already have their own issue in the tracker, I'll add links to these when possible.  

Please provide any additions to this list.  Feel free to share opinions as to the priority of any of these ideas.

### Idea for Py-ART from the conference broken down by area

#### pyart.io

* [x] UF writing (#186, completed in #388) 
* [ ] Reading NEXRAD Level II message 1 format (#352)
* [ ] Reading single field/sweep from NEXRAD Level II
* [ ] Interpolating NEXRAD data to a uniform grid spacing when not-constant (#152, #252)
* [ ] Full support for reading ODIM_H5 files (#131)
* [ ] Writing ODIM_H5 files.

#### pyart.correct

* [ ] Correct unfolded velocities in the region based dealiasing algorithm using a sonde profile or previously unfolded field.
* [ ] Updated phase processing code using self consistency or other additional constraints (#125)

#### pyart.graph
* [ ] Default colormaps based on field name.
* [ ] Default luminosity limits based on field name.
* [ ] Categorical colorbars.
* [ ] Expose calculation of inputs to pcolormesh in the  public API.

#### pyart.grid
* [ ] Three dimensional linear interpolation based gridding as used by SPRINT.
* [ ] Nearest neighbor gridding (#182)
* [ ] Distance weighted gridding where the weighting distance in x, y and z can be specified independently 

#### pyart.retrieve
* [ ] QPE equaltion, R(A), R(KDP), etc.
* [ ] VAD, VVT and DVAD retrievals.  Some of these are available in [SingleDop](https://github.com/nasa/SingleDop)

#### Other 
* [ ] Quasi vertical profiles, QVP.
* [ ] Object for iteration over radar objects by specifying a list of files (useful for QVP).
* [X] Cross section tool for making building RHI like scans from PPI data (completed in #380)
* [ ] Radar composibility (add, remove, insert sweeps or rays) (related #127 and #242)
* [ ] Create conda packages for other radar tools (RSL, wradlib, RadX, PyTDA, SingleDop, ARTView, etc).
* [ ] GridFilter class.
* [ ] GateFilter method to include/exclude based upon height of gate.

#### Documenation
* [ ] Add links to short course materials.

issue_title: OAuth2Session.token_updater callback does not bind session information
issue_body:
I've been looking for a way to persist tokens. The OAuth2Session.token_updater callback seems like the right tool for the job but it omits essential context information such as who is this token bound to? That information appears to be in session._client but there is no way to access that information.

This is especially important for applications which might manage multiple connections and user identities.

To persist a token via the OAuth2Session.token_updater() callback one needs to know to whom that token is bound so that it can be looked up under the (token_url, client_id, username) triplet.

As it stands now it appears as if the token is a single global entity [1], but perhaps I'm missing something, if so I'd appreciate clarification on how to persist tokens.

Also there does not seem to be an API to access the client on the OAuth2Session object, currently it's self._client which is class private.

[1] Yes, I understand the token is bound to the session and client but there is no access to that hence you can only update one global token.

issue_title: LoadTableFromStorageJob:  allow adding sources from blobs, bucket + glob
issue_labels: [{'id': 82126549, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20bigquery', 'name': 'api: bigquery', 'color': 'cd7f32', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2+', 'name': 'priority: p2+', 'color': 'ffee58', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': '8bc34a', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Default the initial `source_uris` to an empty list, then add methods to add extra sources.  E.g.:

``` python
class LoadTableFromStorageJob(_AsyncJob):

    ...

    def __init__(self, name, destination, source_uris=(), client, schema=()):
        super(LoadTableFromStorageJob, self).__init__(name, client)
        self.destination = destination
        self.source_uris = list(source_uris)
        # Let the @property do validation.
        self.schema = schema
        self._configuration = _LoadConfiguration()

    ...

    def add_bucket_source(self, bucket, glob):
        """Add a source URI for blobs matching 'glob' in 'bucket'.

        :type bucket: string or :class:`~gcloud.storage.bucket.Bucket`
        :param bucket: bucket or bucket name

        :type glob: string
        :param glob: blob name or name prefix with '*'
        """
        if isinstance(bucket, Bucket):
            bucket = bucket.name
        self.source_uris.append('gs://%s/%s/' % (bucket, glob))

    def add_blob_source(self, blob):
        """Add a source URI for a single blob.

        :type blob: :class:`~gcloud.storage.blob.Blob`
        :param blob: blob
        """
        bucket = blob.bucket.name
        self.source_uris.append('gs://%s/%s/' % (bucket, blob.name))
```

Yarn (https://yarnpkg.com/) was introduced last November (2016).  Yarn provides advantages over npm to use as a build tool, especially:
  - Yarn provides a repeatable way to lock down dependencies, compares to npm
  - Yarn offline cache solves npm installation issues in a limited network environment
  - Yarn installation is faster with or without cache than npm

Pants should support using Yarn and Yarn lock file as default for node_* targets. This can be done by either move Pants node.js support entirely to Yarn, or provide Yarn as an alternative to npm.

issue_title: Allow pants to select a target by file(s)
issue_body:
There should be an option to accept literal files, and then select the targets that own those files. Similar to how the `--changed-parent` triggers a diff and the targets are selected based on the result.
The proposed syntax is something like:
``` shell
$ ./pants \
  --owner-of=this/is/a/file/name.java \    # flag triggers owner lookup
  --owner-of=this/is/a/file/name/too.py \  # flag triggers owner lookup
  compile                                  # goal
```
I'd be happy to start working on this right away 😄

Any comments, questions and tips on the implementation are welcome and appreciated.
👖 cc'ing @stuhood

issue_title: ExpGraph yields all indexed Addresses, rather than only those directly parsed from input specs
issue_body:
ExpGraph yields an Address for every LegacyBuildGraphNode transitively reachable from the roots... but the contract for `inject_specs_closure` is to only yield Addresses that were directly equivalent to the input Specs.

This is the difference between `self.context.target_roots` (the roots) and  `self.context.targets` (the transitive closure of the roots): what is yielded is currently the latter, but should be the former. The `_index` method still needs to index the transitive closure, but it only needs to yield the roots.

To get the roots of a request, you should be able to simply merge the Address sets computed at the root of the graph. You can visualize something like:
```
./pants run src/python/pants/engine/exp/examples:viz --   tests/python/pants_test/engine/exp/examples/scheduler_inputs list 3rdparty/::
```
to see what I mean.

issue_title: Conv layers overrides data_format passed to the layer with keras config image_data_format
issue_body:
Convolution layers with MXNet backend, uses keras config image_data_format for preparing the kernels. It ignores data_format passed to the layer explicitly.

This is a rare case from usage perspective where users use data_format parameter of the layer. Majority of the users use ~/.keras/keras.json config file to set the image_data_format.

This needs to be fixed. Few test cases in convolutional layer tests needs to be enabled after the fix.
No info on how to use onnx models in the README. At least links on how to get started will be useful.

issue_title: Allow updates to run when a plugin version changes but the main Kolibri version does not
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

Currently database migrations will only happen if an upgrade occurs to the Kolibri version. In the somewhat unlikely event that a plugin version upgrades but the Kolibri version does not (for example in a pip driven installation), this may cause breakage of expected behaviour, as a migration will not be applied.

### Expected behavior

If a plugin version updates, and needs to apply a database migration, the database migration command should be called at Kolibri startup to ensure this happens.

### User-facing consequences

Very low risk possibility of unmigrated databases.

### Context

Follow up from https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/pull/2650

issue_title: Support layer- and interface- prefix for local layers
issue_body: The convention that has come up for repo names for base, interface, and charm layers is to prefix them with ["layer-"](https://github.com/juju-solutions?query=layer-) and ["interface-"](https://github.com/juju-solutions?query=interface-).  When those repos are cloned for local development, you have to be careful to rename the directory to remove the prefix.  It would be nice if `charm build` would handle the prefix automatically.

issue_title: Invalid placement syntax not caught in proof
issue_body:
I'm reviewing a bundle that passes `charm proof` but fails to push to the charm store when the lxd placement directive omits a machine id or unit name.

```bash
$ charm push . cs:~midonet-charmers/bundle/openstack-midonet-liberty
ERROR cannot post archive: bundle verification failed: ["invalid placement syntax \"lxc:\"","invalid placement syntax \"lxc:\"","invalid placement syntax \"lxc:\""]
```

From bundle.yaml:
```yaml
  mysql:
    charm: "cs:trusty/mysql-18"
    num_units: 1
    options:
      "max-connections": 500
    annotations:
      "gui-x": "531"
      "gui-y": "237.2949192431123"
    to:
      - "lxc:"
```
I've verified that `charm push` succeeds if the lxc: placement directive is removed.

issue_title: We need to ping to keep the connection alive in JaaS
issue_body:
The JaaS controller aggressively times us out. We need to ping it in order to keep the connection alive.

`ax.set_xlabel(r'azimuth $\bm{\varphi}$', fontsize=11)`

According to `nn_socket` man page, the function returns file descriptor, so `Socket.sock` attribute should reflect this.

If we run
```
import nnpy 

sub = nnpy.Socket(nnpy.AF_SP, nnpy.PUB)
sub.bind('tcp://*:5555')
print(sub.sock)
```
The `0` will be printed.

`strace` gives the following in its output for above script
```
$ grep -e ^socket -e ^bind log           
socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM|SOCK_CLOEXEC, IPPROTO_IP) = 7
bind(7, {sa_family=AF_INET, sin_port=htons(5555), sin_addr=inet_addr("0.0.0.0")}, 16) = 0
```
So `Socket.sock` should be `7`, not `0`.

issue_title: Trusted pulls from notary server are not supported
issue_body:
This is related to https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/4787.

There doesn't seem to be any way within the Python API to get the equivalent of what the Go CLI supports via the DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST and DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST_SERVER environment variables as described here:

https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/trust/trust_sandbox/

Since docker-compose is based on docker-py, that would seem it can't do trusted pulls. Is that right? If so, that's a fairly serious limitation.

Is there any plan or timeline to add support for notary servers? There is a Python library which implements tuf support (https://github.com/theupdateframework/tuf), so adding support for a notary server to docker-py would seem to be a matter of integrating it the same way the Go bindings are used in docker/cli.

issue_title: Remove client side validation of LogConfig
issue_body:
As @mnowster calls out in https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/1869#issuecomment-131059698 doing validation of things on the client (`docker-py`) means that any time a new log driver is added we have to: add it to the enum, review, create a new release.

It's likely that `docker-py` will always lag behind engine.

We should remove `LogConfigTypesEnum` and just let the server validate it.

issue_title: Connecting to crossbar router started after component regularly fails
issue_body:
**Scenario**
A running application with pretty minimal code to just have a `Component` instance connecting the router.

The router is not running. It is started some seconds after the appliction.

**Expected behavior**
Initially the app obviously fails to connect to the router, when retrying it can eventually connect once the router is up.

**Observed behavior**
In a lot of cases no `join` event is fired at all. the component just stops to reconnect.

Find below the code and the log output.

```python
import os

from autobahn.asyncio.component import Component, run


def create_component():
    authentication = None

    component = Component(
        transports=[
            {
                "type": "websocket",
                "url": os.environ.get("AUTOBAHN_ROUTER", "ws://localhost:8080/ws"),
            }
        ],
        authentication=authentication,
        realm="realm1",
    )

    return component


class Application:
    def __init__(self, component):
        self.component = component

        component.on("join", self.on_join)
        component.on("leave", self.on_leave)

    async def on_join(self, session, details):
        print("JUST JOINED")

    async def on_leave(self, session, reason):
        print("JUST LEFT")


def main():
    component = create_component()

    app = Application(component)

    run([component], log_level="debug")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

issue_title: Coreference resolution model always out of memory with paper recommended params
Relative data path not working in ModelTestCase.set_up_model
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
Getting the FileNotFoundError when was doing pytest. Then I tried allennlp-as-a-library-example and got the same error.

`FileNotFoundError: file tests/fixtures/academic_paper_classifier.json not found`

I found this is caused by the os.chdir() within the set_up_model, so it cannot find the test file relative to current directory anymore.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Checkout allennlp-as-a-library-example and cd to this directory
2. Running pytest
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
pytest pass.

**System (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Linux
 - Python version: 3.6
 - AllenNLP version: master
 - PyTorch version: 0.4.0

issue_title: Wrong build RPM on Oracle Linux
issue_body:
Using Oracle Linux 7.2
platform.dist()[0] on Oracle Linux returns 'oracle', and the condition 

> if distro in ['centos', 'redhat', 'debian', 'fedora']:

 is not satisfied, therefore rpm-package builds without systemd service file.
Please fix it.

issue_title: Better Voice numbers when voices are specified
issue_body:
From Mailing list, September 2016.  When running this code, the voice numbers in the generated MusicXML are very high numbers (probably the memory location of the Voice object stored as an id) -- these should be made much more sane.  Problems w/ MuseScore arise.

```
from music21 import *

newScore = stream.Score()
upperPart = stream.Part()         
lowerPart = stream.Part()

for ind in range(5):
    measUpper = stream.Measure()
    measLower = stream.Measure()

    # Create Voices
    soprano = stream.Voice()            
    alto    = stream.Voice()
    tenor   = stream.Voice()
    bass    = stream.Voice()

    n = note.Note('C5', quarterLength=1)
    soprano.repeatAppend(n, 4)

    n = note.Note('G4', quarterLength=1)
    alto.repeatAppend(n, 4)
    
    n = note.Note('E4', quarterLength=1)
    tenor.repeatAppend(n, 4)

    n = note.Note('C3', quarterLength=1)
    bass.repeatAppend(n, 4)

    measUpper.insert(0,soprano)
    measUpper.insert(0,alto)
    
    measLower.insert(0,tenor)
    measLower.insert(0,bass)
    
    upperPart.append(measUpper)    
    lowerPart.append(measLower)

newScore.insert(0,upperPart)
newScore.insert(0,lowerPart)
upperPart.insert(0,clef.TrebleClef())
lowerPart.insert(0,clef.BassClef())

newScore.write('musicxml','test4p.xml')
```

issue_title: Port functionality from a number of integration tests back to their unit tests using mocked responses
issue_body:
As I split unit tests and integration tests for #130 the amount of test coverage for a number of clients fell significantly due to it now being tucked away in the integration tests directory. Some key examples being:

- `integration_test_caiso.py`
- `integration_test_ercot.py`
- `integration_test_nyiso.py`
- `integration_test_pjm.py`

There may possibly be others. We should coverage from a number of these test methods back into unit tests, using mocked requests rather than real requests. **Note:** By porting them back we should _not_ make network requests but should instead mock responses for URLs that we expect the test to request.

I created this Terraform provider plugin for Deis out of need, would love for the Deis community to pick it up and help me out on maintaining it. If we can get enough stars and stability we can get it committed to the offical Terraform repository so that it's built-in.

It would be very convenient to have an https log drain type. Log aggregation providers like SumoLogic do not provider managed syslog collectors, preferring instead https based collectors. While Sumo does offer self-hosted syslog collectors, it seems very awkward to run a non-Deis managed container on each CoreOS cluster node, and pipe journal messages in for each application, or alternatively consume all journal messages, with no application prefixes. Using a drain to an https collector endpoint seems like a much more elegant solution.

issue_title: Customizing deis router times out at times
issue_body:
I have a customised deis-router in order to run it as a reverse proxy for some of the apps I have deployed on deis. I have taken the code from deis-router from the 1.2.1 release. It works but at times it times out on servicing the requests. The requests doesn't seem to have forwarded to the other apps(which is again uwsgi servers configured to accept http data). Currently it is still under development and so there is isn't much traffic for the timeouts to occur. Any suggestions to improve this?

What I have really done is just to modify nginx templates with my custom rules. I haven't changed anything else. I attempted to upgrade confd to a newer version(it is 5.0 in the router) but couldn't get it to work so stuck with the old one deis used.

$ globus ls --long af7bda53-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec:/~/myproject

issue_title: settings_new.from_columns not sticking strictly to given feature names
issue_body:
I am trying to extract _strictly the same features for test data which tsfresh has selected from extracted ones for training data_. I have this as code: ``` settings_new.from_columns(trained_features)``` [learnt from [here]](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/issues/22)
Code snippet:
```python
X = extract_features(train_df, column_id='unit', column_sort='cycles', feature_extraction_settings=extraction_settings, parallelization='per_sample')
X_filtered = select_features(X, y)
trained_features = list(X_filtered.columns.values)[1:]
settings_new.from_columns(trained_features)
X_new = extract_features(test_df, column_id='unit', column_sort='cycles', feature_extraction_settings=settings_new, parallelization='per_sample')
```
**Problem:** My test features count to 5328, same for X, and not X_filtered which is 1045.
Perhaps, all parameters are being tweaked again, or I am doing it the wrong way. So, how should I get only those features from test data which have been selected finally from test data feature extraction & selection process.
OS: Ubuntu 16.04 LTS
tsfresh 0.7.0

P.S.: Sorry for the long post, I just wanted to be clear. I have asked this on gitter too, I hope that is not arrogant. Thanks a lot for this real-needed library!

issue_title: Allow NaN or None values to be passed in, and silently ignored
issue_body:
In my DataFrames, I oftentimes find it very reasonable to have quite a few NaN or None values. 

In order to run tsfresh, I just set all those values to 0, which is... not ideal. Even imputing missing values would not work particularly well for several of my use cases. 

Yet, it seems (from a super naive outsider's perspective), like this is filtering that tsfresh could do relatively easily itself. 

When it grabs each time_series, it can simply remove or categorically ignore NaN/None, and compute features on the values that do exist. This makes my life easier when, say, one customer signs up a month after another customer, and thus has missing values for that month. 

Again, super naive outsider perspective here, I know this might be impossible. But if it is possible, I'd love to add in that bit of filtering!

issue_title: conda doesn't update an updatable package?
issue_body:
With conda 4.1.1 I'm observing the below behaviour:
```
λ conda update --all
<SNIP>
The following packages will be DOWNGRADED:

    dynd-python:       0.7.2-py35_1233        dynd/channel/dev       --> 0.7.2-py35_0
    numba:             0.27.0.dev-np110py35_6 numba                  --> 0.26.0-np110py35_0
    sphinx:            1.4.1-py35_0                                  --> 1.3.4-py35_0         dhirschfeld
```
Why does an upgrade downgrade packages? If a new package had a hard dep on an earlier package, fair enough, but both the newer numba and newer sphinx should also satisfy constraints so shouldn't be touched.

Even though I haven't included the numba channel conda should see that a newer version is already installed than what it can see in the defaults so should just leave it alone if it also satisfies all constraints.

Below I show that conda refuses to update sphinx even though it will happily do so if I explicitly specify the version which implies that the newer version must also satisfy the constraints so should have been chosen when the original update was requested
```
λ conda update sphinx

Fetching package metadata .................
Solving package specifications .............

# All requested packages already installed.
# packages in environment at C:\Python:

issue_title: Conda numpy fail to deep reload in IPython.
issue_body:
Not a big deal, just the IPython's test suite fails on conda-installed numpy, with :

```
 File "~/dev/ipython/IPython/testing/decorators.py", line 212, in skipper_func
    return f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "~/dev/ipython/IPython/lib/tests/test_deepreload.py", line 38, in test_deepreload_numpy
    dreload(numpy, exclude=exclude)
  File "~/dev/ipython/IPython/lib/deepreload.py", line 341, in reload
    return deep_reload_hook(module)
  File "~/dev/ipython/IPython/lib/deepreload.py", line 311, in deep_reload_hook
    newm = imp.load_module(name, fp, filename, stuff)
  File "~/anaconda/lib/python3.5/imp.py", line 244, in load_module
    return load_package(name, filename)
  File "~/anaconda/lib/python3.5/imp.py", line 214, in load_package
    return _exec(spec, sys.modules[name])
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 626, in _exec
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap_external>", line 662, in exec_module
  File "<frozen importlib._bootstrap>", line 222, in _call_with_frames_removed
  File "~/anaconda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/numpy/__init__.py", line 214, in <module>
    if sys.version_info[0] >= 3:
NameError: name 'sys' is not defined
```

Likely because of the `__ini__.py` difference:

```diff
l111
+ import ctypes
+ _old_rtld = sys.getdlopenflags()
+ sys.setdlopenflags(_old_rtld | ctypes.RTLD_GLOBAL)
+
l234
+
+ sys.setdlopenflags(_old_rtld)
+ del _old_rtld
+ del sys
+ del ctypes
+ __mkl_version__ = '11.3.3'
```

Not that I think deep reload is something that works, or should work, or is even desirable, but it is is a difference of behavior in between the two numpy versions, so not sure it is expected.

issue_title: Close with delete aliases does not correctly close non alaised indices
issue_body:
Suppose you have three indices 
1. foo
2. bar
3. baz

that match a Curator filter of some kind and are actionable under the close action.

Suppose as well you have one alias `bang` that points at indices `foo` and `bar`.

Suppose you perform a `close` action on these indices. Indices `foo` and `bar` will be closed; `baz` will not, because it doesn't have an alias.

This seems counter intuitive to me. It seems like Curator should delete an alias if it exists with this option, but continue with regularly scheduled programming if one does not.

issue_title: Issue running pre-commit when specifying an array of `types`?
issue_body:
Consider the following config:

```yaml
repos:
-   repo: https://github.com/prettier/prettier
    sha: 1.10.2
    hooks:
    -   id: prettier
        name: prettier (markdown)
        files: ''
        types: [markdown]
    -   id: prettier
        name: prettier (javascript + markdown)
        files: ''
        types: [javascript, markdown]
```

Only the first hook matches anything. The second hook does not:

```bash
╔═ mark@mark: ~/vpg/repro git:(master) virtualenv:(venv)
╚═ ♪ pre-commit run -a
prettier (markdown)......................................................Failed
hookid: prettier

Files were modified by this hook. Additional output:

README.md 134ms

prettier (javascript + markdown).....................(no files to check)Skipped
```

(Full repro: https://github.com/magicmark/pre-commit-issue-repro)

Am I doing something wrong in the second example?

Thanks!

As a safety net, we've started running pre-commit on CI, e.g.
```
git diff --name-only "$GIT_COMMIT"^..."$GIT_COMMIT" | xargs pre-commit run --files
```

one of our projects uses `no-commit-to-branch` and we just noticed this behavior on CI:
```
Don't commit to branch...................................................Failed
hookid: no-commit-to-branch

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/var/lib/jenkins/.cache/pre-commit/repoVUdrA6/py_env-python2.7/bin/no-commit-to-branch", line 11, in <module>
    load_entry_point('pre-commit-hooks==1.1.0', 'console_scripts', 'no-commit-to-branch')()
  File "/var/lib/jenkins/.cache/pre-commit/repoVUdrA6/py_env-python2.7/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pre_commit_hooks/no_commit_to_branch.py", line 22, in main
    return int(is_on_branch(args.branch))
  File "/var/lib/jenkins/.cache/pre-commit/repoVUdrA6/py_env-python2.7/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pre_commit_hooks/no_commit_to_branch.py", line 10, in is_on_branch
    branch = cmd_output('git', 'symbolic-ref', 'HEAD')
  File "/var/lib/jenkins/.cache/pre-commit/repoVUdrA6/py_env-python2.7/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pre_commit_hooks/util.py", line 28, in cmd_output
    raise CalledProcessError(cmd, retcode, proc.returncode, stdout, stderr)
pre_commit_hooks.util.CalledProcessError: (('git', 'symbolic-ref', 'HEAD'), 0, 128, u'', u'fatal: ref HEAD is not a symbolic ref\n')
```

most likely this is not really an issue with `pre-commit` but with the way our Jenkins jobs are set up (?). I worked around this by using `SKIP=no-commit-to-branch pre-commit run` but just thought I would let you know.

issue_title: Always use keyword-only style for optional arguments
issue_body:
Let's assume we have a public API function with the following signature: `def f(arg, flag1=None, flag2=True, flag3=False)`.
User is supposed to call it as `f('arg', flag2=False)`.
But now it might do it as `f('arg', None, False)` which makes a mess.

I suggest changing all such signatures to `def f(arg, *, flag1=None, flag2=True, flag3=False)`.
It forces user to specify optional args by name only.

@achimnol ?

Currently all the LDAP interactions happen in a synchronous fashion, which blocks the entire server while waiting for a response from the LDAP server. This will cause issues if the LDAP server takes a while to respond and/or a lot of LDAP logins happen at the same time.

A relatively simply fix for this should be to use the twisted [deferToThread](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/api/twisted.internet.threads.deferToThread.html) API every time we call a function on `ldap3`

issue_body: With several people outside the core group writing toil workflows, we need to outline some advice, tips, standards, etc for developers

> POST /api/hosts

total 32

-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1809 Dec  6 06:06 cert2.pem
